raw_point: The engine is designed to present teaching units, not excerpts as ends in themselves.
why_routed_there: This is the anchor for ranking item (4) first and for defining teaching-unit language.

raw_point: Not keeping the original text untouched is catastrophic because it causes wrong attribution to scholars.
why_routed_there: This is the anchor for ranking item (1) second and for defining corruption/false attribution as catastrophic.

raw_point: Under the ordinary reading, weak item (2) is recoverable because I can manually look things up.
why_routed_there: This is the main reason item (2) sits below items (4) and (1) under the ordinary reading.

raw_point: If item (2) means pieces were cut out, then it becomes absolutely deadly.
why_routed_there: This is the key caveat that prevents overclaiming the tail of the ranking.

raw_point: A too-big excerpt causes chaos in the owner’s mind: topics get mixed and scrambled.
why_routed_there: This is the anchor for treating item (3) as granularity fitness rather than simple shortness.

raw_point: External context and metadata can surround the raw excerpt text as part of the teaching unit.
why_routed_there: This is the distinction that prevents item (1) from being misread as forbidding all external support layers.
